examples.title: Scan-Line Polygon Filling Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to fill interior pixels of a polygon using horizontal scan lines
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ScanLineFill(Polygon)
BEGIN
 1. Find Ymin and Ymax of polygon
 2. FOR y = Ymin to Ymax DO
 3. Find intersection points of scanline y with polygon edges
 4. Sort intersection points in increasing x-coordinate
 5. Pair intersection points (P1,P2), (P3,P4), ...
 6. FOR each pair (P_left, P_right) DO
 7. FOR x = P_left.x to P_right.x DO
 8. Set pixel (x,y) to fill color
 9. END FOR
 10. END FOR
 11. END FOR
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm processes the polygon scanline by scanline, finding where each horizontal line intersects polygon edges. Pairs of intersections define regions to fill inside the polygon.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n + m) where n=polygon edges, m=scanlines

examples.title: Z-Buffer Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for hidden surface removal using depth values
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ZBuffer(Scene)
BEGIN
 1. Initialize frame buffer with background color
 2. Initialize Z-buffer with maximum depth (far plane)
 3. FOR each polygon in scene DO
 4. FOR each pixel (x,y) covered by polygon DO
 5. Calculate depth z at (x,y)
 6. IF z < Z-buffer[x,y] THEN
 7. Z-buffer[x,y] = z
 8. FrameBuffer[x,y] = polygon color
 9. END IF
 10. END FOR
 11. END FOR
 12. Display FrameBuffer contents
END
examples.explanation: Z-buffer stores depth for each pixel. When rendering, if new surface is closer (smaller z) than stored depth, update both buffers. At end, frame buffer contains visible surfaces.

examples.title: Basic 2D Point Clipping Algorithm
examples.description: Cohen-Sutherland line clipping algorithm for viewport boundaries
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CohenSutherland(Line, Window)
BEGIN
 1. Assign region codes to both endpoints
 2. IF both codes are 0000 THEN
 3. Accept entire line
 4. ELSE IF codes have common bit THEN
 5. Reject line (completely outside)
 6. ELSE
 7. Find intersection with window boundaries
 8. Replace endpoint outside with intersection point
 9. Recursively process updated line
 10. END IF
END

FUNCTION ComputeCode(Point, Window)
BEGIN
 code = 0000
 IF x < x_min THEN code[0] = 1
 IF x > x_max THEN code[1] = 1
 IF y < y_min THEN code[2] = 1
 IF y > y_max THEN code[3] = 1
 RETURN code
END
examples.explanation: Uses 4-bit region codes to quickly determine if lines are completely inside, completely outside, or partially visible. Efficiently clips lines to rectangular viewport.
